name: Batch Data Processor V2 — Bounded Generic Statistics
scenario: Upgrade the Batch Data Processor from Topic 7.4 (defensive programming) with a fully generic statistics engine. The new engine uses `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>` to compute statistics on any numeric type — salary data, stock prices, order amounts, scores — all with the same generic code.
description: A generic statistics library for numeric data processing. `DataBatch<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>` holds a batch of numbers and computes comprehensive statistics. `BatchProcessor` runs multiple batches and produces reports.

core.description:
`DataBatch<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>` class:
- Stores a named batch of values (List<T>, String batchName)
- `add(T)`, `addAll(List<T>)`
- `count()`, `sum()`, `average()`, `min()`, `max()`, `range()` — all properly typed
- `isBetween(T value)` — is value within [min, max]?
- `percentile(double p)` — return the pth percentile element (sort, get index p/100 * count)
- `toString()` — formatted summary

Generic static `BatchProcessor` methods:
- `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> DataBatch<T> merge(List<DataBatch<T>> batches, String name)` — combine all batches into one
- `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> List<T> outliers(DataBatch<T> batch, double threshold)` — values where |value - avg| > threshold
- `<T extends Comparable<T>> T maxAcrossBatches(List<DataBatch<T>> batches)` — global max across all batches

Demonstrate with three use cases:
1. Exam scores (Integer) — 3 batches (classes), merged, outliers, 75th percentile
2. Stock prices (Double) — 2 batches, merged, global max
3. Same DataBatch<Integer> and DataBatch<Double> coexist — showing type safety
Each DataBatch.toString() prints: 'BatchName: count=N, avg=X.X, min=Y, max=Z'
Zero casts. All generic. Null-safe (return Optional or throw for empty batches).

bonus.description: Add `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> boolean isNormal(T value, DataBatch<T> batch, double sigmas)` — is value within sigmas standard deviations of mean? Requires computing std dev.
Add `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> Map<String, Double> summary(List<DataBatch<T>> batches)` — returns Map<batchName, average> for all batches using generic methods.
Add `<T extends Comparable<T>> DataBatch<T> topN(DataBatch<T> batch, int n)` — new batch with just the top N elements by value.

example_output:
=== Exam Score Analysis ===
Class A: count=8, avg=83.75, min=68, max=95
Class B: count=8, avg=80.25, min=62, max=96
Class C: count=8, avg=90.13, min=86, max=95
All Classes: count=24, avg=84.71, min=62, max=96
75th percentile: 92
Outliers (threshold=15): [62, 96]

=== Stock Price Analysis ===
Tech: count=4, avg=2078.0, min=412.0, max=3890.0
Finance: count=4, avg=1347.0, min=620.0, max=2200.0
Global max price: 3890.0
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. The dual bound `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>` is the core challenge. percentile(): sort a copy, return element at index (int)(p/100 * count). outliers(): |item.doubleValue() - average()| > threshold. merge(): create new DataBatch, addAll from each batch in list.
submission_instructions: Save as BatchProcessor.java. Push to GitHub. Comment: 'Generic statistics engine — works for any numeric type'.
tips_for_success: DataBatch<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> — declare both bounds on the class
percentile(double p): sort a copy, index = (int)(p / 100.0 * count()), return sorted.get(index)
merge: new DataBatch<>(name), then for each batch call result.addAll(batch.data)
maxAcrossBatches: find max element across all batches — only needs Comparable (not Number)
concepts_reinforced: Generic static methods on non-generic utility class
Same generic class serving Integer and Double
